ab: Claqi nami tiping cida, mneniq nami alang Toda han, prading ku musa muyas sapah pyasan daw, musa nami thdil Hori da, ana dhuq saya; ida to mniq alang mkbrnux ka kndusan nami.
ch: 小時候我們家住在都達，上學以後，就搬到了埔里，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Toda . After I started school, we moved to Puli and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: Babaw mnusa ku muyas tubeyhing sapah pyasan daw, prading ku smluhay slhayan wowaya Sediq tnpusu ka yaku da, wada ku mkla ou balay kkluwan waya Sediq tnpusu Taiwan.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: So kiya, sngayan rbawan kngkawas ni cida, musa nami smiling rdrudan alang ka yami qbsuran mu snaw, iyux nami mkla wowaya Sediq ta nanaq.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: Mspac kmxalan ka kngkawas rudan ni da, binaw qtan u mkkray balay ka hnyuwan niya, so hari mrisaw na.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: Slingan mu ka hiya, piya kluwan kari ka klaun na rmngaw, mndungus balay ka rrngaw na kari Sediq Toda, ana kari Bunun, kari Hakka mi kari Tanah Tunux uri pa, klaun na rmngaw kana.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的賽德克族都達說得很流利，也會說布農語、客家話和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Toda, as well as Bunu, Hakka and Japanese.

ab: Smling ku hiya da u hana ku mkla, kla uri laqi cida ka hiya pa tna sneluhay kari Tanah Tunux da, qridin na u  Bunun, kiya mi mniq qpahun na u wada ppkla hbaraw Hakka, so kiya hay mkla rmngaw ou kluwanan kari ka hiya.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是布農族人，而他工作上認識許多客家人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Bunu, and he knows many Hakkas at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: Psiling nami kari cida pa, ou balay prngawan nami quri pkla waya knkla pnsltudan tnpcamac ka drudan.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: Ana wada ini klayi hari mbahang duma ka kari niya, binaw wada nami dmuwi qnawan ddilun psueyga, malu mu ndwanun mbahang dungan.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: Claqi ku cida, ini mu hari klayi ka wewaya mu Sediq nanaq, maku ku msiqa hari hyuwan mu nanaq sediq tnpusu.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: Kndudul ou ka klaun mu da u, wada ku krinah qmita Sediq Balay ka hnyuwan mu nanaq pa, tnnanak balay kiya, niqan ririh na pskuraw mu balay uri.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為賽德克族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Sediq is unique and something to be proud of.
